title: <div>&quotTell mommy I'm buying one, so I'm going to need some of my money,&quot
content: "Tell mommy I'm buying one, so I'm going to need some of my money," he said--at first sternly--then adding: "Is that okay?" He quickly smiled without meaning to, momentarily embarking upon a false sense of momentary manliness: cocking the right bit of his lip like the hammer of a gun--but he dared not place his finger on the trigger of that smile as he knew his mother truly had the final say about how and what he did with all the pieces of his life, his money being a rather large piece. This chain link smile was hurriedly erected to keep his sister from stumbling upon the awareness of how much he begged and wished to be free from all of them. "As long as you pay her what's due, I don't think she'll mind," his sister said while exhaling two lungs worth of marijuana smoke into all the space between them. Her hand sliced through the cloud as she passed the glass pipe back to him. "Yeah, I'm sure she won't mind. If I pay her."
context: From document: "Tell mommy I'm buying one, so I'm going to need some of my money…
